reviews.author: The MY World
reviews.text: It's night market. It's not cheap I think, but the kinds of seafoods are a lot here. So It's good choice for dinner. Also they have many other things also. I recommend to come at least one time. And better to look for the paid parking to avoid unnecessary things.

reviews.author: Anchal T
reviews.text: This market is more of a food destination than a shopping market. It’s lively and bustling, with a huge seafood section offering fresh fish, prawns, crabs, and local specialties. Food lovers will enjoy exploring the variety of street food and seafood options. Shopping choices are limited—mostly fake Crocs and a few souvenirs, so don’t expect much for serious shopping. Overall, it’s a good place to experience the local food scene and seafood market atmosphere rather than shop.

reviews.author: Jaroslav PTAAH
reviews.text: ⭐⭐⭐☆☆ Chợ đêm Phú Quốc – your nose will remember this place An interesting spot that’s definitely worth seeing at least once. On a very small area, an impressive number of smells compete for your attention — grilled seafood, sweet desserts, mystery sauces, and a few aromas science still hasn’t fully explained 😄 It’s lively, colorful, and full of energy, and yes, it’s something you should experience. That said, once is probably enough. After one round, you’ve seen it, smelled it, and mentally checked the box. Summary: fun, chaotic, and unforgettable… mostly for your nose. Worth one visit, no strong need for a sequel.

reviews.author: Carmen Stephens
reviews.text: This place is so fun. They have matching outfits! Food! Beer! Knock off items! The list goes on. It’s also not too big so it’s a quick hour that fun. I don’t think I would recommend eating there but def do some shopping.

reviews.author: Chirag Vyas
reviews.text: Wonderful experience of people shopping in the streets of Phu Quoc which is open the entire Night. From different kinds of delicious food to precious pearl jewellery, you get anything and everything here. Loaded with a lot of people visiting this place from different parts of the world, you have to walk long lanes to see its beauty. The best part is, you wont be tired at all as you can take their massages to keep you relaxed and soothing Must visit

reviews.author: Louie
reviews.text: If you’re in Phu Quoc, you have to check out this night market! It’s always lively and packed with locals and tourists every evening. There are endless food stalls serving super fresh seafood, tropical fruits, and tasty street snacks. You can also find souvenirs, handmade crafts, and clothes at good prices. The atmosphere is energetic but chill — perfect for a relaxed evening stroll. Definitely one of the must-visit spots on the island!

reviews.author: Veggie Greek Goddess
reviews.text: Honestly this is maybe the best night market I've been to, also considering some famous ones from Thailand. They have a lot of exotic fruit stalls, some pretty cool souvenir shops and overall the vibe is nice and it feels safe and not too intense.

reviews.author: adva krohn
reviews.text: Really nice night market with lots of shops, restaurants, and food stalls - both sweet and savory! There's a great variety and a fun atmosphere. Prices are a bit on the higher side, but still reasonable for what you get. Definitely worth a visit!

reviews.author: Otakar Klika
reviews.text: A must-visit while you’re on Phú Quốc! This night market is full of energy, colors, and amazing smells. You’ll find tons of souvenirs, local products, and especially a huge variety of street food – from fresh seafood to sweet treats, tropical fruits, nuts, and freshly squeezed juices. Prices are great, and in some stalls, you can even try a bit of bargaining. It’s the perfect place to grab a bite, do some shopping, and soak in the lively local atmosphere. Whether you’re hungry or just browsing, this market’s got something for everyone.

reviews.author: Rajeev Jha
reviews.text: Nice place for shopping also try some foods only if you. An eat sea foods not much for vegetarian for sure Yes, plenty of places to shop items souvenir and dresses glasses clothes bags n all but not genuine may be copy n all

reviews.author: D B
reviews.text: A wide variety of goods, including great pearls at excellent prices. The food is amazing, with delicious pancakes and a lot of seafood. There’s something for everyone. The massages are top-notch, with many well-executed options. The atmosphere is fantastic, capturing the essence of a lively night market. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Calvin Wong
reviews.text: Phu Quoc's Biggest Night Market - Worth a Visit But Not Essential This bustling night market offers a typical yet enjoyable experience. While packed with seafood restaurants, avoid dishes displayed on ice as they're likely less fresh. Look for the unique water coconuts - harder to open but prized for their meat rather than just the juice. Several souvenir shops sell wooden items with optional name customization. A tempting chestnut stall fills the air with its aroma, though I didn't try it. While it's a solid night market experience, it doesn't stand out significantly from others in Southeast Asia. Worth visiting if you're nearby, but not a must-see if you've experienced similar markets elsewhere.

reviews.author: Militaru Marius
reviews.text: A lot of people, a lot of noise, it's worth a visit, especially for a table with

reviews.author: Pamela
reviews.text: Definitely must go. Lots of food stalls, restaurants, and souvenirs. They pretty much sell all the same thing. Just a matter of who is willing to bargain for a sale. Outside of the market are plenty of restaurants, bars, massage parlors, and much shopping.

reviews.author: Luc Diefenbach
reviews.text: Night market was great! I honestly love walking through these. It’s always so chaotic and fun to walk through getting yelled at to eat there food or get a drink. The seafood here is incredible and some of the best I have ever had. I absolutely ate so much walking through here. This place was amazing and i definitely recommend checking it out. You won’t be disappointed.!

reviews.author: Abin K Antony
reviews.text: The night market features numerous shops offering a wide variety of food items, fruits, crafts, souvenirs, clothing, and more. The food selection includes an impressive range of meats and seafood. The prices at the shops were comparatively higher, especially at the crafts and souvenirs shops.

reviews.author: Reha Sehgal
reviews.text: Chợ đêm Phú Quốc is a lively and colorful night market that’s perfect for exploring local life and trying a variety of street food. The atmosphere is vibrant, with plenty of stalls selling seafood, snacks, souvenirs, clothes, and handmade goods. The market is busy but manageable, and walking around gives a real sense of the local culture. The food is fresh and tasty, with lots of options for seafood lovers and casual bites. Prices are reasonable, though a little bargaining can help. While it can get crowded during peak hours, the energy and variety make it worth a visit. Chợ đêm Phú Quốc is a must-visit for travelers wanting to enjoy authentic local food, shop for souvenirs, and experience the lively evening culture of Phu Quoc.

reviews.author: Niranjan Pai
reviews.text: Famous Phu Quo Night market famous for yummy street food,and many more

reviews.author: Kathy E. Sanati
reviews.text: I really loved my experience at this market. I took the free Vinbus 17 and got off a block away. Fruits were cheaper here. I tried avocado ice cream here for the first time and it was the best ice cream I ever had. Highly recommend this for anyone visiting Phu Quoc. I only made it here once but next time I will try to come often to try all the different foods.

reviews.author: L Y
reviews.text: 2025.8.4 It’s very touristy here. Reminds me of Myeongdong. A good walk around after dinner. I personally am wary of getting fleeced here. We had our dessert here. That’s about it !

reviews.author: Vichuda Prom
reviews.text: Phu Quoc night market is an attractive destination that you should not miss when traveling to the pearl island. Here you can "immerse" yourself in a culinary paradise, comfortably shop and eat in the bustling atmosphere. The market is a merger of Dinh Cau Night Market and the old Bach Dang Night Market, the most bustling and crowded entertainment, dining and shopping spot. The market lights up at 6pm every day (although many stalls are still open from 3pm), but from 8am onwards is the busiest time. If you arrive late at the night market and are worried about what time Phu Quoc night market opens, don't worry, the market doesn't close until midnight, around 11am, 12pm, depending on the number of tourists. If you go with a large group, you should buy a little of each dish and share it so you can enjoy all the delicious dishes.Food safety and hygiene at the market is always a concern for those with stomach problems, so remember to choose restaurants that look clean and tidy. Not always true but usually the restaurant that is crowded is good, you just go there to eat and it's ok. Before buying an item or eating something, remember to ask the seller for the price first, to avoid getting ripped off after eating or choosing to buy. At Phu Quoc night market as well as many markets in tourist destinations, there are often poor quality, fake, counterfeit goods… remember to choose carefully.

reviews.author: Coralie Pattenden
reviews.text: A bright and colourful market with lots of different food options and souvenirs. They have a lot of delicious seafood which is cooked fresh. Try to avoid getting a taxi from outside the market as they can charge you whatever they like. We ordered a grab taxi and walked a bit further away to meet up with them because it'd be nearly impossible to meet up at the entrance.

reviews.author: Nazeeliya Nas
reviews.text: Some street food , rolled ice creams and lots of souvenirs. But I felt like the prices were high compared to Ben thanh market of ho chi minh city. So if you’re planning to go to the city , better to buy magnets and other souvenirs from there.

reviews.author: Czt
reviews.text: I recommend the market, it has souvenirs and a great variety of seafood at very good prices. It's not a big deal to spend an hour or two there, it's fine. And above all, an incredible variety of very pretty pearl jewelry from the area.

reviews.author: Md Shariar Shanaz Shuvon (Shuvonsec)
reviews.text: A must-visit spot in Phu Quoc! The night market is full of energy, colors, and delicious smells. You’ll find everything from fresh seafood to souvenirs and local snacks. I recommend trying the coconut ice cream — super refreshing in the hot weather. Prices are fair if you compare and ask nicely. Best time to go is after 7 PM when it gets lively but not too crowded yet. It’s not just for shopping — the whole vibe is fun. Music, lights, people… it feels like a festival every night. I visited both day and night, but night is where the real magic happens.

reviews.author: Robyn “Tweezledinky”
reviews.text: The touristic street was full of life and fantastic shellfish and stalls of all kinds. Over the bridge at the local market we found a much more real shellfish and fish market. This was my favourite part. There was no smell. Everything was fresh and looked so pretty.

reviews.author: Ron “Ronster” 荣少
reviews.text: Vibrant night market near the sea. Lots of different types of shops here, from clothings to handy-crafts to food, there will be lots of choices. Clothing wise there won't be much variety as the shops here sell mainly beach wear and the Hawaiian" feel type of warm weather clothes. In terms of seafood there are many restaurants here selling them but I would recommend going to Lobster cave, a 5min walk away from the exit of the night market. The coconut ice cream shop with the green tables is delicious. Lots of massage shops here, but I didn't have a chance to give them a go. Overall I had a great time here.

reviews.author: Efchen
reviews.text: Love night markets in Asia, where everything is so vivid but sometimes it gets a bit annoying if people try too hard to get customers. So much food and fruits, for someone coming from Europa it is kind of a paradise.

reviews.author: Tatjana Mijailovic
reviews.text: A little bit touristy but definitely worth the visit! There's plenty of good food to choose from. Lots of sight and smells will attract you to each of the food stalls that line this food street. There are also lots of spa places around. It's most lively at night. I definitely recommend trying the coconut ice-cream and seafood!

reviews.author: Saviour Tom
reviews.text: Amazing night market experience with incredible variety! This place is a food lover's paradise with so many unique options to try. Food Highlights - Tried exotic meats like frog and crocodile - surprisingly delicious! - The roasted peanuts coated with sugar are addictive - Incredible selection of exotic seafood - don't miss out on trying something new - The ice cream rolls made fresh with milk and real fruit are a must-try. The lychee flavor was absolutely amazing! Shopping & Crafts - Found a talented artisan making customizable crochet items on the spot - Another vendor creates personalized passport covers - great for unique souvenirs Perfect for wandering around and discovering new flavors and crafts. The atmosphere is lively and there's something interesting around every corner. Great mix of local food culture and artisan crafts. Highly recommend spending an evening here exploring all the different stalls.
